children.text: The “South Cow Mountain OHV Management Area”, as generally depicted on the map.
The “North Cow Mountain Recreation Area”, as generally depicted on the map.

children.heading: Legal descriptions; correction of errors
children.label: b Legal descriptions; correction of errors

children.heading: Preparation and submission
children.label: 1 Preparation and submission
children.text: As soon as practicable after , the Secretary of the Interior shall prepare a map and legal descriptions of the boundaries of the recreation area. The Secretary shall submit the map and legal descriptions to the Committee on Resources of the House of Representatives and to the Committee on Energy and Natural Resources of the Senate.October 17, 2006

children.heading: Legal effect
children.label: 2 Legal effect
children.text: The map and legal descriptions of the recreation area shall have the same force and effect as if included in this Act, except that the Secretary may correct clerical and typographical errors in the map and legal descriptions. The map shall be on file and available for public inspection in appropriate offices of the Bureau of Land Management.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary of the Interior shall administer the recreation area in accordance with this section and the laws and regulations generally applicable to the public lands, including the Federal Land Policy and Management Act of 1976 ( et seq.).43 U.S.C. 1701

children.heading: Existing rights
children.label: 2 Existing rights
children.text: The establishment of the recreation area shall be subject to all valid existing rights.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary of the Interior shall continue to authorize, maintain, and enhance the recreational use of the land included in the recreation area, including motorized recreation, hiking, camping, mountain biking, sightseeing, and horseback riding, as long as such recreational use is consistent with this section and other applicable law.

children.heading: Off-road and motorized recreation
children.label: 2 Off-road and motorized recreation
children.text: Motorized recreation shall be a prescribed use within the South Cow Mountain OHV Management Area, occurring only on roads and trails designated by the Secretary for such use, except as needed for administrative purposes or to respond to an emergency. Nothing in this paragraph shall be construed as precluding the Secretary from closing any trail or route from use for purposes of resource protection or public safety.

children.heading: Mountain biking
children.label: 3 Mountain biking
children.text: Mountain biking shall be a prescribed use within the recreation area, occurring only on roads and trails designated by the Secretary for such use. Nothing in this paragraph shall be construed as precluding the Secretary from closing any trail or route from use for purposes of resource protection or public safety.

children.heading: Access to private property
children.label: e Access to private property
children.text: The Secretary of the Interior shall provide any owner of private property within the boundaries of the recreation area adequate access to the property to ensure the reasonable use and enjoyment of the property by the owner.

children.label: B
children.text: exchange with a willing party, as expressed in a written agreement between the Secretary and the party; or

children.label: C
children.text: purchase from a willing seller, as expressed in a written agreement between the Secretary and the seller.

children.heading: Administration of acquired lands
children.label: 2 Administration of acquired lands
children.text: Lands or interests in lands within or adjacent to the boundaries of the recreation area that are acquired by the Bureau of Land Management, and title or possession of which is vested in the United States after , shall be managed by the Secretary as part of the recreation area.October 17, 2006

children.heading: Adjacent management
children.label: g Adjacent management
children.text: Nothing in this section creates protective perimeters or buffer zones around the recreation area.
